description: M-26-14 Appendix B.3 timestamp accuracy check. Compares event @timestamp against ingest time; when absolute skew meets/exceeds threshold_seconds (default 300), persists m_26_14.skew_seconds and m_26_14.skew_exceeded:true on the document. Below threshold, nothing is stored (storage-frugal by design). Never fails ingestion: parse errors are swallowed. Wire it from logs@custom or a per-dataset @custom pipeline -- see README.md.

_meta.maps_to: Appendix B.3 (accurate timestamps); Log Management element
_meta.version_notes: Portable across 8.19 and 9.4. This is the core mechanism; the system integration ntp data stream (9.2+) is an optional supplement.

script.source: if (ctx['@timestamp'] == null || ctx['_m_26_14_ingest_time'] == null) { return; } ZonedDateTime evt; ZonedDateTime ing; try { evt = ZonedDateTime.parse(ctx['@timestamp'].toString()); ing = ZonedDateTime.parse(ctx['_m_26_14_ingest_time'].toString()); } catch (Exception e) { return; } long skew = ChronoUnit.SECONDS.between(evt, ing); if (Math.abs(skew) >= params.threshold_seconds) { def m = (ctx.containsKey('m_26_14') && ctx['m_26_14'] instanceof Map) ? ctx['m_26_14'] : new HashMap(); m.put('skew_seconds', skew); m.put('skew_exceeded', true); ctx['m_26_14'] = m; }
